alternatives.path: native reasoning plus repository truth and Git posture
alternatives.failurePropagation: conversation state can drift when repository evidence is absent

alternatives.path: current Codex thread, workspace, Skill loader, and runtime handoff affordances
alternatives.failurePropagation: host or loader behavior can fail silently and is not portable evidence

alternatives.path: installed 847-byte handoff Skill
alternatives.failurePropagation: thin packets may omit authority, evidence, freshness, or resume checks

alternatives.path: intent-contract -> repository truth -> handoff -> closure-contract
alternatives.failurePropagation: a missing upstream contract or stale repository snapshot weakens downstream closure

alternatives.path: cskwork/handoff-skill; planning-with-files only as a heavy reference
alternatives.totalCost: low for handoff content; high for planning-with-files system
alternatives.failurePropagation: replacement can create duplicate handoff authority; heavy persistence and Hooks can widen failure scope

alternatives.path: repository-owned state schema, Git snapshot, continuation bootstrap, and deterministic resume checks
alternatives.failurePropagation: bad schema ownership or stale generation can institutionalize incorrect state

alternatives.path: project-owned transition and continuation contract
alternatives.failurePropagation: premature universal wording can encode one host's semantics as a cross-agent rule

alternatives.path: owner confirms consequential phase exit, authority transfer, and acceptance
alternatives.failurePropagation: unclear ownership or rubber-stamp review leaves state ambiguous

alternatives.path: consumer-owned advisory transition reminder
alternatives.totalCost: persistent event, context, debugging, and host-maintenance cost
alternatives.failurePropagation: event mismatch or automatic persistence can inject stale state or mutate beyond authority

comparisons.comparisonOutcome: The current composed path is the minimum sufficient path for bounded repository work, while carrier-neutral interruption and re-entry behavior remains unproven.

residualGap.reason: No reproducible paired behavior demonstrates material failure of the current composed path or material advantage from replacement.
nextEvidence: paired interruption and re-entry fixtures for current native-plus-installed composition
content-level comparison against the pinned richer handoff body without installing or executing it
freshness, authority-transfer, no-delta, and honest phase-exit assertions

alternatives.path: native intent interpretation and Skill matching
alternatives.failurePropagation: implicit misses and false matches may remain unobservable

alternatives.path: Codex visible Skill inventory and official plugin cache
alternatives.totalCost: enumeration, context, startup, and update cost not measured
alternatives.failurePropagation: root precedence or runtime drift can invalidate static inventory claims

alternatives.path: capability-router
alternatives.failurePropagation: router absence or stale projection can degrade selection but must not grant authority

alternatives.path: intent-contract -> capability-router -> domain capability -> closure-contract
alternatives.failurePropagation: contract drift can cascade, while explicit reroute checkpoints contain later damage

alternatives.path: planning-with-files as a routing/persistence comparison system
alternatives.totalCost: high executable, Hook, persistence, and host-variant surface
alternatives.failurePropagation: parallel control planes can conflict with the canonical router and closure authority

alternatives.totalCost: medium-to-very-high tooling, dependency, service, and supply-chain cost
alternatives.failurePropagation: manager or projection bugs can fan out across every consumer root

alternatives.path: stable IDs, source pins, root precedence, collision policy, capability profiles, and deterministic projection checks
alternatives.totalCost: medium initial schema and verification cost
alternatives.failurePropagation: bad standards affect all consumers but can be versioned, tested, and rolled back

alternatives.path: configuration, security, and runtime owners decide trust, permissions, collisions, and persistent activation
alternatives.failurePropagation: incorrect approval can authorize broad root or account changes

alternatives.path: advisory capability-ecosystem recall Hook
alternatives.totalCost: persistent host-specific event and context rent
alternatives.failurePropagation: false reminders or event incompatibility add noise and can mask router-loading causes

comparisons.comparisonOutcome: The routing chain and repository governance cover policy structure; the unresolved issue is live-state, collision, portability, and cost evidence, not a proven missing Skill body.

residualGap.reason: Static root counts and public tooling features do not reproduce a material routing failure or prove that another manager is needed.
nextEvidence: read-only root activation and precedence probes in the current host
same-name collision fixtures with explicit expected resolution
implicit and explicit routing cases with false-positive, latency, context, permission, and maintenance-cost observations

alternatives.path: native reasoning distinguishes fact, inference, uncertainty, and claim scope
alternatives.failurePropagation: fluent unsupported judgment can be mistaken for evidence

alternatives.path: runtime permissions, source access, and verification tools
alternatives.failurePropagation: tool success can be misreported as domain acceptance

alternatives.path: closure-contract
alternatives.failurePropagation: mechanical closure can overclaim if domain evidence is absent

alternatives.path: intent-contract -> domain work -> deterministic verification -> closure-contract
alternatives.failurePropagation: bad intake or evidence classification contaminates later closure

alternatives.path: no external candidate search authorized for this lane
alternatives.failurePropagation: a generic Skill could falsely displace accountable authority

alternatives.path: deterministic tests, provenance records, independent review workflow, and veto-capable acceptance gates
alternatives.failurePropagation: invalid fixtures or captured reviewers create false assurance

alternatives.path: risk-to-proof ladder and cannot-close policy owned by the affected project
alternatives.failurePropagation: generic thresholds can become unsafe outside their jurisdiction or domain

alternatives.path: competent, independent, accessible, veto-capable, accountable reviewer or affected-subject authority
alternatives.failurePropagation: missing competence, independence, access, or veto makes review ceremonial

alternatives.path: review reminder Hook
alternatives.failurePropagation: a reminder can be mistaken for completed or competent review

comparisons.comparisonOutcome: This lane is covered by layered process controls plus accountable human or domain authority; its non-delegable authority boundary is not an external Skill gap.

residualGap.reason: No general capability can manufacture missing professional, organizational, legal, safety, privacy, or affected-subject authority.
nextEvidence: bind the concrete domain, affected subjects, consequence, evidence owner, reviewer competence, independence, access, veto, and acceptance authority when a consequential case arises

alternatives.path: repository truth, Git history, and explicit owner judgment
alternatives.failurePropagation: manual edge reconstruction can miss consumers or stale projections

alternatives.path: official baselines and current runtime metadata
alternatives.failurePropagation: ecosystem drift can stale compatibility assumptions

alternatives.path: no single Skill owns source-to-consumer lifecycle governance
alternatives.totalCost: low apparent cost but high omission risk
alternatives.failurePropagation: text-only guidance can diverge from manifests and actual consumers

alternatives.path: intake -> provenance/security/portability review -> admission -> projection -> consumer verification -> lifecycle feedback
alternatives.failurePropagation: a skipped gate can contaminate downstream release and consumer projections

alternatives.failurePropagation: incorrect central edges propagate broadly but remain versionable and testable

alternatives.failurePropagation: collapsed ownership can turn evidence transport into unauthorized mutation

alternatives.path: consumer-owned drift or projection reminder
alternatives.totalCost: persistent host coupling and alert maintenance
alternatives.failurePropagation: stale or broad automation can overwrite consumer state or imply authority

comparisons.comparisonOutcome: The durable carrier is likely project-owned governed data plus verification tooling, not another Skill body; complete live source-to-consumer evidence is still missing.

residualGap.reason: The comparison identifies an evidence and lifecycle-observability need, but no reproducible fixture yet proves which existing governance invariant fails or what minimum implementation closes it.
nextEvidence: source-to-curation-to-projection-to-consumer fixture graph with typed authority and verification edges
freshness, supersession, rollback, public-private, and failure-propagation mutation tests
consumer-owned read-only confirmation before any cross-repository or standards claim

preferredCurrentDirections: retain native-first composed transition and routing paths
treat EL-03 as a non-delegable human or domain-authority boundary
prefer governed project data and verification tooling over a new Skill body for EL-04
collect bounded behavior, collision, cost, and projection evidence before reconsidering any carrier
batchDecision.nextGate: bounded behavior-and-cost evidence design for EL-01 and EL-02 plus projection-fixture evidence design for EL-04 before any residual-gap, repository-authoring, adaptation, or Hook decision

claimLimits: This comparison completes the selected demand batch's architecture comparison; it does not exhaust every STM, P, or SG coordinate.
Static source review and local metadata do not prove live candidate value, current root activation, cross-host parity, or a material residual gap.
No third-party candidate is approved, executable, installed, adapted, vendored, or selected for release.
No Skill, Hook, project standard, consumer projection, cross-repository write, runtime mutation, or remote push is authorized by this record.
recheckTriggers: paired behavior or cost evidence reproduces a material failure in the preferred current path
a candidate's pinned source, license, security, portability, architecture, or maintenance state changes
the target host, model, loader, roots, permissions, workspace, consumer, or authority boundary changes
a residual-gap, repository-authoring, adaptation, Hook, standard, or cross-repository decision is proposed
